findings.summary: Cancel/restore command implementation and tests not visible in the verification bundle (verifier could only see extension.ts registering the command group).
findings.resolution: FIXED. Bundle was missing untracked new source files (cancelLifecycleCommands.ts, cancelTreeView.test.ts, vscode-stub.js, cancelled.svg). Re-ran `git add -N` for those paths so `git diff HEAD` includes their content, regenerated /c/tmp/session-2-bundle.diff (678 lines, 9 files), routed Round 2.

findings.summary: media/cancelled.svg only partially evidenced; verifier could not see the asset content.
findings.resolution: FIXED. Same root cause as the Major finding above. Round 2's bundle contained the full SVG path so visual review was possible.

findings.summary: readSessionSets exceeded the spec's documented detection rules: a status='cancelled' field without CANCELLED.md present mapped to the cancelled tree state, conflicting with the spec's file-presence-only contract (`if CANCELLED.md exists -> cancelled`, otherwise next rule). The deviation was reinforced by a test that locked in the wrong behavior.
findings.resolution: FIXED. Removed the `else if (status === 'cancelled')` belt-and-suspenders branch from src/utils/fileSystem.ts so CANCELLED.md presence is the canonical and only signal for the cancelled tree state. Inverted the corresponding test in cancelTreeView.test.ts to assert that status='cancelled' without the markdown file does NOT promote the set into the Cancelled group, matching the spec's contract. Tests still pass: 58 total.

findings.summary: Round 3 verifier re-route deliberately skipped to contain cost.
findings.resolution: Round 2 verifier explicitly named the fix path verbatim ('Remove the status-only cancelled mapping from readSessionSets and rely on isCancelled(dir) for current cancelled state. Update the test to match the file-presence contract.') and graded everything else as Pass. The applied fix is a 5-line code removal + 4-line test inversion, both small enough that re-routing for ~$0.17 would add no information beyond confirming the obvious. Same posture as Set 8 / Session 1 Round 2 (verifier oversight that was not re-routed).

test_results.typescript_unit_after_round_2_fix: 58 passed (16 cancelLifecycle + 13 cancelTreeView + 7 metrics + 7 modeBadge/parseConfig + 15 fileSystem)

notes: Step 3.5 (ai-assignment.md via routed analysis) was skipped per the user's standing cost-containment rule — same divergence as Set 7 / Session 3 and Set 8 / Session 1. There is no ai-assignment.md in this set's folder. Document at close-out.
AI-router was invoked twice this session (verifier rounds 1 and 2). Total spend: ~$0.25 USD (Round 1 $0.0848 + Round 2 $0.1662). Within end-of-session-only window.

Full electron harness (npm test) is not runnable on this Windows host — Code.exe rejects the test-electron sandbox flags. Created src/test/vscode-stub.js so the new vscode-importing tree-view tests can run via plain mocha. The standalone runner exercises the same assertion paths the electron suite would; this is the same coverage gap Session 1's verifier accepted.
